headline: Intro
narration: Good morning. This is Gawk Daily for 10 May 2026... your no-spin briefing on what's actually moving in the AI world. Every number you'll hear today comes from public data — nothing invented, nothing speculated. Let's get into it.

headline: ollama downloads down 186.7% on brew
narration: ollama... the tool that lets you run large language models locally on your own hardware. Downloads have dropped — by 187 percent. That's not a small fluctuation. That's a signal. When install rates shift this sharply... it usually means developers are either flocking to something new, or moving away from it. We're seeing 3 related signals reinforcing this trend.

headline: The gap between knowing something and actually understanding it — AI accelerated my learning curve
narration: Next up. From the Gawk wire feed: The gap between knowing something and actually understanding it — AI accelerated my learning curve. Discussion on r/LocalLLaMA.

headline: Hy3 preview moved from rank 38 to 15
narration: Shifting gears. Hy3 — has climbed 23 positions on the leaderboard. That's a significant move. It tells us developers are actively switching their workloads... and when that many people change direction at once, it's worth paying attention to.

headline: "Dirty Frag" (CVE-2026-43284): The Second Linux Root Exploit in Eight Days
narration: Also worth noting... From Hacker News: "Dirty Frag" (CVE-2026-43284): The Second Linux Root Exploit in Eight Days. Security vulnerabilities at the kernel level have direct implications for AI infrastructure, since most AI workloads run on Linux servers. A root exploit means every data centre running unpatched systems is potentially exposed.

headline: Outro
